description: Thin rope or thick string; flexible line for tying or binding. 'Nira' names what ties near—the medium-weight line for everyday binding.
sound_symbolism: The word binds near. 'Ni' draws close in binding, cord tightening. Then 'ra' reaches along the length, connection extending.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo nira' (the cord). Between 'sima' (thread) and 'suro' (rope) in thickness. For tying packages, lacing, general binding.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Ties that bind gently—cord connects without restricting, holds without trapping.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Cord held life together—tying bundles, securing loads, the humble line of daily work.
